title: Cross-Timezone Night Burst: Fraudster's Active Hours Override Cardholder Geography
narrative: Compromised card shows sudden activity concentrated between 2-6 AM cardholder local time (derived from addr1 timezone), but precisely aligned to 8 PM-midnight in a different region. Pattern emerges when card1_txn_count_24h spikes from baseline 0-2 to 8-12 transactions, all occurring within attacker's evening hours. Transactions cluster in 15-45 minute windows with TransactionAmt ranging $47-$189, targeting digital goods (ProductCD=W) and electronics merchants. The is_night flag triggers consistently while dist1 shows stable billing location. Card historically dormant during these hours over prior 90 days, suddenly executing CNP purchases with consistent P_emaildomain patterns (burner email services). The temporal signature reveals fraudster working their daytime/evening shift while cardholder sleeps, often targeting merchants in intermediate timezones where fraud controls relax during off-peak staffing.
indicators: All transactions occur 2-6 AM cardholder timezone (addr1-derived) over 3-7 consecutive nights
Transactions align precisely to 8 PM-midnight window in suspected fraudster timezone (Eastern Europe/South Asia pattern)
card1_txn_count_4h increases from 0 baseline to 4-7 transactions per night window
Historical analysis shows zero transactions in this time window over prior 90 days
is_night=1 for 100% of transactions despite card's previous daytime-only pattern
P_emaildomain switches to disposable/burner services coinciding with temporal shift
txn_hour clusters tightly (variance <1.2 hours) across multiple days, suggesting scripted automation
summary: Card suddenly active 2-6 AM in cardholder's timezone after 90 days of daytime-only use, with transactions tightly clustered in 15-45 minute bursts targeting digital goods and electronics. The timing precisely matches evening hours (8 PM-midnight) in a different region, suggesting a fraudster working their own schedule while the victim sleeps.
reasoning: Legitimate cardholders don't suddenly reverse their sleep patterns and make multiple purchases at 3 AM using burner emails. The consistent night-time clustering across consecutive days, combined with the shift to disposable email domains, indicates a criminal operating from a different timezone who has compromised the card and is executing scripted purchases during their own waking hours.
indicator_explanations: Card suddenly active only during cardholder's deep sleep hours across multiple nights
Activity pattern matches fraudster working hours in distant overseas timezone
Card goes from silent to burst of transactions in tight four-hour windows
This time window was completely unused by legitimate cardholder for three months
Card now exclusively transacts at night despite zero historical nighttime activity
Email provider changes to throwaway service exactly when sleep-hour attacks begin
Transaction times repeat with robotic precision suggesting automated script not human behavior
